title: छिन्नमस्ता स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीछिन्नमस्तास्तोत्रम् ॥

श्रीछिन्नमस्ताया नमः।

प्रचण्डचण्डिकां देवीं छिन्नमस्तां नमाम्यहम्।
स्वशिरश्छेदिनीं घोरां रक्तधाराप्रवाहिनीम्॥१॥

वज्रवैरोचनीं विद्यां प्रचण्डोग्रस्वरूपिणीम्।
रतिमन्मथसंस्थां च प्रणमामि पुनः पुनः॥२॥

डाकिनीवर्णनीयुक्तां रक्तधारात्रयोज्ज्वलाम्।
कबन्धधारिणीं देवीं छिन्नमस्तां नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

मुक्तकेशीं दिगम्बरां सर्पकुण्डलभूषिताम्।
मुण्डमालाविभूषाढ्यां ध्यायामि जगदम्बिकाम्॥४॥

वामे डाकिनिकां देवीं दक्षे वर्णनिकां तथा।
मध्ये छिन्नशिरां देवीं त्रिमूर्तिं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥५॥

त्रिसन्ध्यां रक्तपानेन तर्पयन्तीं स्वयोगिनीः।
स्वमस्तकं छेदयित्वा वज्रेश्वरीं नमाम्यहम्॥६॥

सूर्यमण्डलसंकाशां तेजोरूपां महोज्ज्वलाम्।
योगिनीचक्रमध्यस्थां छिन्नमस्तां भजाम्यहम्॥७॥

षष्ठी सा महाविद्यानां प्रचण्डा छिन्नमस्तका।
त्यागमोक्षस्वरूपां तां वन्दे योगमयीं शिवाम्॥८॥

समस्तबन्धविच्छेदकारिणीं मोक्षदायिनीम्।
आत्मत्यागबलोपेतां छिन्नमस्तां नमाम्यहम्॥९॥

कुण्डलिनीजागरणार्थकारिणीं
सुषुम्नामार्गप्रचालिनीं ताम्।
षट्चक्रभेदनकरीमनन्तां
छिन्नमस्तां प्रणमामि नित्यम्॥१०॥

छिन्नमस्तास्तवं नित्यं यः पठेत्सुसमाहितः।
सर्वबन्धविनिर्मुक्तो मोक्षमार्गं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्रीछिन्नमस्तास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Chinnamastaa Stotram ||

Shri Chinnamstaayaa Namah.

Prachandachandikaam Deveem Chinnamastaam Namaamyaham.
Svashirash-Chhedineem Ghoraam Raktadhaarapravahineem.||1||

Vajravairoochaneem Vidyaam Prachandograsvaroopineem.
Rati-Manmatha-Samsthaam Cha Pranamami Punah Punah.||2||

Daakinee-Varnaree-Yuktaam Raktadhaaratrayojjvalaam.
Kabandhadharineem Deveem Chinnamastaam Namaamyaham.||3||

Muktakesheem Digambaraam Sarpakundalabhooshitaam.
Mundamaalaa-Vibhooshaadhyaam Dhyaayaami Jagadambikaam.||4||

Vaame Daakinikaam Deveem Dakshe Varnanikaam Tathaa.
Madhye Chinnashiraam Deveem Trimoortim Pranamaamyaham.||5||

Trisandhyaam Raktapaanena Tarpayanteem Svayogineeh.
Svamastakam Chhedayitvaa Vajreshvareem Namaamyaham.||6||

Sooryamandalasamkaashaam Tejoroopaam Mahojjvalaam.
Yoginee-Chakra-Madhyasthaam Chinnamastaam Bhajaamyaham.||7||

Shashthi Saa Mahavidyaanaam Prachandaa Chinnamastakaa.
Tyaagamokshasvaroopaam Taam Vande Yogamayeem Shivaam.||8||

Samastabandha-Vichchheda-Kaarineem Mokshadaayineem.
Aatmatyaaga-Balopetaam Chinnamastaam Namaamyaham.||9||

Kundalinee-Jaagaranaarthakaarineem
Sushumnamaargaprachaalineem Taam.
Shatchakrabheda-Nakareem-Anantaam
Chinnamastaam Pranamami Nityam.||10||

Chinnamastaa-Stavam Nityam Yah Pathet-Susamaahitah.
Sarvabandhavinimurkto Mokshamaargam Sa Gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Chinnamastaa Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री छिन्नमस्ता को नमस्कार।

१. प्रचण्ड चण्डिका देवी छिन्नमस्ता को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ — स्वयं अपना शिर छेदने वाली, घोर, रक्त-धारा प्रवाहित करने वाली।

२. वज्रवैरोचनी विद्या, प्रचण्ड-उग्र-स्वरूपिणी, रति-मन्मथ पर स्थित — बार-बार प्रणाम करता हूँ।

३. डाकिनी-वर्णिनी सहित, तीन रक्त-धाराओं से उज्ज्वल, कबन्ध धारण करने वाली देवी छिन्नमस्ता को नमस्कार।

४. मुक्तकेशी, दिगम्बरा, सर्प-कुण्डल-भूषित, मुण्डमाला-विभूषित — जगदम्बिका का मैं ध्यान करता हूँ।

५. वाम में डाकिनी देवी, दक्ष में वर्णिनी, मध्य में छिन्नशिरा देवी — इस त्रिमूर्ति को प्रणाम करता हूँ।

६. त्रिसन्ध्या में रक्तपान से अपनी योगिनियों को तर्पित करती, स्वमस्तक छेदकर — वज्रेश्वरी को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ।

७. सूर्यमण्डल के समान, तेजोरूपा, महोज्ज्वल, योगिनी-चक्र के मध्य स्थित — छिन्नमस्ता को मैं भजता हूँ।

८. महाविद्याओं में षष्ठी, प्रचण्डा छिन्नमस्तका — त्याग-मोक्ष-स्वरूपा, योगमयी शिवा को वन्दन।

९. समस्त बन्धनों का विच्छेद करने वाली, मोक्षदायिनी, आत्म-त्याग के बल से युक्त — छिन्नमस्ता को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ।

१०. कुण्डलिनी जागरण करने वाली, सुषुम्ना मार्ग में प्रचालित करने वाली, षट्चक्र भेदन करने वाली अनन्ता — छिन्नमस्ता को मैं नित्य प्रणाम करता हूँ।

जो एकाग्रचित्त होकर नित्य छिन्नमस्ता-स्तव का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वबन्धनों से विनिर्मुक्त होकर मोक्षमार्ग पर जाता है।

viniyog.beej: श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं ऐं वज्रवैरोचनीये हूं हूं फट् स्वाहा
viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीछिन्नमस्तास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य पुराणोक्त ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् एवं उपजाति छन्दः। श्री छिन्नमस्ता (दश महाविद्या) देवता। श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं ऐं वज्रवैरोचनीये हूं हूं फट् स्वाहा बीजम्। आत्मत्याग शक्तिः। सर्वबन्धविच्छेद एवं मोक्ष कीलकम्। श्रीछिन्नमस्ताप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Chinnamasta is the 6th Mahavidya — the self-decapitating Goddess of supreme self-sacrifice
Symbolizes the awakening of Kundalini and piercing of the six chakras
Invokes liberation from all bonds — material, emotional, and karmic
Represents the ultimate act of selfless giving — feeding others with one's own life-force
Powerful for Kundalini yoga practitioners and spiritual seekers
Traditionally recited for removal of ego (ahamkara) completely through the symbolism of self-decapitation
Invokes fearlessness and detachment from worldly illusions
description: The Chinnamasta Stotram, known in Sanskrit as छिन्नमस्ता स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Durga. This revered text originates from Tantrasara. Devotees recite this prayer to chinnamasta is the 6th Mahavidya — the self-decapitating Goddess of supreme self-sacrifice and represent the ultimate act of selfless giving — feeding others with one's own life-force. It is also said to destroy ego (Ahamkara) completely through the symbolism of self-decapitation. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Navratri. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Chinnamasta Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Chinnamasta Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Ten verses to the 6th Mahavidya for Kundalini awakening, liberation, and bondage destruction.
relatedSearches: Chinnamasta Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit Devanagari PDF
Chinnamasta Devi 6th Mahavidya story and meaning
Vajra Vairochani mantra Om Shrim Hrim Klim Aim

Tantric diksha required for Chinnamasta mantra

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chinnamasta Stotram and which Mahavidya does it glorify?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chinnamasta Stotram is a ten-verse Sanskrit hymn dedicated to Goddess Chinnamasta, the sixth of the ten Mahavidyas (Dasha Mahavidya) in the Shakta tantric tradition. The hymn opens with prachanda-chandika devim chinnamastam namamyaham and is preserved in the Tantrasara compendium and several Shakta tantric anthologies. Chinnamasta is depicted as the self-decapitated goddess holding her own severed head, with three streams of blood from her neck feeding her two yoginis Dakini and Varnini and her own severed head — a powerful image of the simultaneous nature of life, death and self-sacrifice. (Sources: Wikipedia Chhinnamasta; David Kinsley's Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine.)

seoFaqs.question: Does reciting the Chinnamasta Stotram require tantric initiation?
seoFaqs.answer: An important traditional distinction must be made. The full Chinnamasta mantra-sadhana — including the sixteen-syllable Vajra-Vairochani mula mantra and accompanying yantra worship — is a strict tantric upasana that requires diksha (initiation) from a qualified guru in the Shakta or Shri-Vidya lineage and is not undertaken without preparation. The stotra itself, however, being a devotional praise hymn (stuti), is generally considered open for sincere devotees to recite as bhakti, similar to other Mahavidya stutis. Devotees seeking to enter the deeper sadhana are advised to approach an authentic acharya rather than self-initiate from internet sources. (Sources: traditional tantric guru-paramparas; Vedadhara Mahavidya guidance.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the spiritual symbolism of Chinnamasta's self-decapitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Chinnamasta's iconography of holding her own severed head while her two attendants and her head itself drink the blood streaming from her neck is one of the most striking images in Hindu tantra. Traditional commentaries interpret it on multiple levels: the absolute self-sacrifice of the divine to nourish creation, the awakening of the Kundalini shakti rising through the sushumna nadi and piercing the chakras, the simultaneous existence of life, death and sexuality (she stands on the embracing couple of Rati and Kamadeva), and the cutting through of the ego (ahamkara) that is the prerequisite for liberation. The stotra explicitly invokes her as kundalini-jagaranartha-karinim and shatchakra-bhedana-karim. (Sources: Kinsley Tantric Visions; Hindupedia Chhinnamasta.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is the most famous Chinnamasta temple located?
seoFaqs.answer: The most famous Chinnamasta temple is the Rajrappa temple located on the confluence of the Damodar and Bhairavi rivers in Ramgarh district of Jharkhand, where the goddess is worshipped as the presiding Mahavidya. Other important Chinnamasta shrines include the Chintpurni temple in Una district of Himachal Pradesh, where the deity is identified with Chinnamastika, and a Chinnamasta shrine within the Kamakhya temple complex in Assam, which houses all ten Mahavidyas. These pithas are major centres of tantric pilgrimage especially during Navratri and Chinnamasta Jayanti on Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi. (Sources: Wikipedia Rajrappa; Wikipedia Chintpurni.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Chinnamasta worshipped and what offerings are made?
seoFaqs.answer: Chinnamasta Jayanti is observed on the Chaturdashi of the bright half of Vaishakha month and is the principal festival of the goddess. She is also worshipped during both annual Navaratris (Chaitra and Ashvina) as the sixth-day Mahavidya, and on Tuesdays and Saturdays which are sacred to the fierce forms of the Devi. Traditional naivedyam includes red hibiscus flowers, kumkum, red cloth, sesame seeds, jaggery and in tantric tradition vegetarian or symbolic substitute offerings — animal sacrifice has been progressively replaced by coconut and pumpkin (kushmanda) bali in most contemporary temples. (Sources: Drikpanchang Mahavidya Jayantis; Rajrappa temple guides.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Chinnamasta connected to the Buddhist goddess Vajrayogini?
seoFaqs.answer: Indologists and tantric scholars have noted a striking iconographic parallel between Hindu Chinnamasta and the Vajrayana Buddhist goddess Chinnamunda Vajrayogini, who is depicted with the identical self-decapitation imagery. Both traditions place the goddess on a copulating couple, both show three streams of blood feeding two attendants and the severed head itself, and both interpret the image as the cutting of the ego and the awakening of the inner channels. Scholars such as Elisabeth Anne Benard have documented the shared tantric matrix from which both forms emerged in eastern India around the eighth to tenth centuries. (Sources: Benard Chinnamasta The Awful Buddhist and Hindu Tantric Goddess; Wikipedia Vajrayogini.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti of Chinnamasta Stotram promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti of the stotra states that one who recites this Chinnamasta-stava daily with concentration becomes free from all bondages (sarva-bandha-vinirmukto) and proceeds on the path to liberation (mokshamargam sa gachchhati). Traditional commentaries extend these benefits to the awakening of Kundalini through the six chakras, complete dissolution of ego-identification, fearlessness in the face of death, victory over enemies both internal and external, and ultimate self-realisation (atma-sakshatkara). For tantric initiates, the stotra is the daily devotional anchor of a much larger sadhana-kalapa. (Sources: Tantrasara; traditional Mahavidya commentaries.)
